title: T-112 — PARITY: Gate screen components — browser screenshot parity vs prototype gate-screens.jsx:106-616
prompt:
# T-112 — Browser parity for the Gate screen components

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §17.6, §17.7
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Parent root: T-061
Depends on: T-111 (wiring)
Prototype reference: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/gate-screens.jsx:106-616

## Goal
Run Playwright screenshots of the production gate route and compare structurally/visually/interactively to the prototype gate-screens.jsx:106-616 covering all four components (GateChecklistPanel, AdvanceGateModal, GateApprovalModal, ApprovalHistoryTimeline). Run axe-core on the route and on each modal-open state; expect 0 violations.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/tests/e2e/npd-gate-screens.spec.ts (Playwright) navigates to /projects/[id]/gate/[gateId]; takes screenshots for: panel default, panel with blockers, AdvanceGateModal open, GateApprovalModal open (approve step), GateApprovalModal open (e-sig overlay), ApprovalHistoryTimeline with eSigned expanded.
2. Each screenshot is checked into _meta/parity-artifacts/01-npd/gate-screens/.
3. axe-core run against the loaded route and again after opening each modal; expect 0 violations.
4. Parity checklist file _meta/parity-artifacts/01-npd/gate-screens/PARITY-CHECKLIST.md ticks structural/visual/interaction/data parity rows against prototype lines.

## Files
- apps/web/tests/e2e/npd-gate-screens.spec.ts [create]
- _meta/parity-artifacts/01-npd/gate-screens/PARITY-CHECKLIST.md [create]
- _meta/parity-artifacts/01-npd/gate-screens/*.png [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given Playwright executes npd-gate-screens.spec.ts, when each screenshot is captured (panel default, panel-with-blockers, AdvanceGateModal, GateApprovalModal-decision, GateApprovalModal-esig, ApprovalHistoryTimeline-expanded), then artifacts are saved under _meta/parity-artifacts/01-npd/gate-screens/ and committed.
2. Given the parity checklist runs against prototype gate-screens.jsx:106-616, when structural/visual/interaction/data parity rows are evaluated, then all rows are ticked or have a recorded deviation note with PRD justification.
3. Given axe-core runs against the loaded route + each modal-open state, when results are collected, then there are 0 violations on the route and 0 violations on each modal open state.

## Test strategy
- Run 'pnpm --filter web exec playwright test npd-gate-screens'
- Run axe via @axe-core/playwright integrated in the spec.

## Risk red lines
- Do not change component or wiring files under this task (T-107..T-111 own those).
- Do not skip axe checks on modal-open states.
